evidence.text: Revenue decreased $393.9 million, or 6.3%, in 2023 compared to 2022. This decrease resulted from a 9.0% decrease in LTL tonnage per day, which was primarily due to decreases in LTL shipments per day and LTL weight per shipment.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to significant decreases in LTL tonnage and shipments.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Our LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 3.5% in 2023 as compared to 2022. Excluding fuel surcharges, LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 8.3% in 2023 as compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has successfully increased LTL revenue per hundredweight, indicating strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Revenue decreased $393.9 million, or 6.3%, in 2023 compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, as evidenced by a 6.3% revenue decrease.

evidence.text: LTL shipments decreased 6.3% in 2023 compared to 2022, and LTL tons decreased 9.3%. LTL tonnage per day decreased 9.0%.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining across shipments and tons, indicating negative volume trends.

evidence.text: The cost of diesel fuel, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both the average price per gallon and consumption. Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel decreased 19.8% in 2023 as compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, particularly diesel fuel, have declined significantly, providing cost relief.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: Productive labor costs, which include wages for drivers, platform employees, and fleet technicians, increased as a percent of revenue to 23.6% in 2023 from 22.9% in 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing as a percentage of revenue, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio increased to 72.0% in 2023 from 70.6% in 2022. Net income decreased by 10.0% in 2023 compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing due to an increased operating ratio and reduced net income.

factors.summary: Cost actions are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages, and benefits decreased $87.2 million, or 3.2%, in 2023 as compared to 2022. Operating supplies and expenses decreased $134.6 million, or 15.8%, in 2023 as compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost actions have delivered measurable savings in salaries and operating expenses.

evidence.text: Our platform and P&D shipments per hour and P&D stops per hour improved during 2023 as compared to 2022, but our linehaul laden load average declined due to the decreased operating density associated with the decrease in our LTL tons.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed productivity improvements and declines indicate no clear trend.

evidence.text: Our linehaul load factor declined due to decreased operating density associated with the decrease in our LTL tons.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining capacity utilization suggests operational challenges.

evidence.text: Despite the decrease in our LTL tons, we maintained a commitment to providing superior customer service to support the continued improvement in our yield. We continued to focus on controlling our costs in the low volume environment, but we continued to invest in new capacity in anticipation of long-term growth in our market share.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cautious management with strategic investments indicates a balanced view.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include continued softness in the domestic economy.

evidence.text: Our financial results for 2023 reflect continued softness in the domestic economy that contributed to the decline in our revenue.

We believe depreciation costs will continue to increase in future periods based on our 2024 capital expenditure plan. While our investments in real estate, equipment, and technology can increase our short-term costs, we believe these investments are necessary to support our continued long-term growth and strategic initiatives.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic investments for growth.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and volume are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: The cost of diesel fuel, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both the average price per gallon and consumption. Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel decreased 19.8% in 2023 as compared to 2022.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivity to fuel prices and volume without clear impact.

evidence.text: The decrease in salaries and wages was due primarily to decreases in the average number of active full-time employees during the year, as we balanced our workforce to align with our customers' shipping trends.

Our employee benefit costs increased as a percent of salaries and wages to 37.5% in 2023 from 36.2% in 2022. The increase in employee benefit costs as a percent of salaries and wages was primarily due to an increase in our employee group health benefit costs that resulted from higher costs per claim.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable labor relations with minor cost increases.
